posts.title: Margins for Memory
posts.excerpt: On leaving blank space beside every sentence — not to decorate, but to remember what the sentence is trying to reach.

posts.title: On Quiet Proofs
posts.excerpt: A proof can be loud, or it can be quiet. The quiet ones leave room for the reader to arrive at the theorem on their own terms.

posts.title: Notebook After Midnight
posts.excerpt: Small scripts that live in the margin of an Obsidian vault — half notebook, half sampler, none of them production-grade.

posts.title: A Syntax for Rain at 02:00
posts.excerpt: A tiny sequencer that treats a list of words like bars in a loop. Not useful. Possibly quiet enough to count.

posts.title: Footnotes for a Half-Remembered Proof
posts.excerpt: A reconstruction of a theorem I half-remember from a late lecture, with all its honest gaps left in place as footnotes.

posts.title: Listening to Nujabes as a Writing Tool
posts.excerpt: A short note on how certain loops force a sentence to slow down — and what prose can borrow from a four-bar phrase.
